dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.remark: Interessante è l’equilibrio tra gli stilemi della utility “fashion” degli anni ‘40 e i canoni degli ‘80. L’essere un capo couture è dato dall’effetto di opulenza , evidenziato ai lati della silhouette e sul dietro, suggerito dalla stola.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.acquisition: Spazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.description: Giacca in jersey ; monopetto; revers a uomo; spalle importanti di linea arrotondata. Vestibilità asciutta al punto vita, sottolineato da un taglio orizzontale;lunghezza ai fianchi; allacciatura a un bottone; tasche rettangolari applicate; cintura dello stesso tessuto, con profilo e fibbia ricoperti in faille di seta. Gonna in faille di seta e jersey di lana accoppiati e impunturati ; struttura a due teli,linea asciutta; lunghezza al ginocchio e cinturetta in vita. T-shirt in jersey di lana; girocollo bordato (cm 1) in faille di seta; maniche corte; lunghezza ai fianchi;punto vita sottolineato da un taglio orizzontale; chiusura con zip sul dorso. Stola in jersey e faille abbinati e impunturati; composta da un grande rettangolo i cui lati corti sono ripiegati verso il centro e fissati in modo da creare un passaggio per le braccia.

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.remark: The winning element here is the very Ferré masculine/feminine material mix where delicate tone-on-tone beading (tunic) contrasts playfully with a zigzag pattern (coat).
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.description: Coat in gray/black zigzag wool. Knee-length kimono style cut with hood, capacious sleeves, slanted besom pockets (4 cm). Wool/silk edging on hood and front. Tunic in white silk georgette. Straight 7/8 cut with wide round neck, short sleeves. White jet beading all over. Skirt in wool/silk satin with gorgeous sheen. Tubular style, knee length.

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.remark: This outfit offers a glimpse of Ferré’s architectural training. Essential to giving extra volume to the jacket is the basic geometric design, as what creates the effect of (in reality non-existent) sleeves is the round piece of fabric folded over and kept in place by artful stitching. Another geometric shape – the semi circle – adds volume to the front of the dress through the use of a belt.

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.remark: The sense of illusion is clear: what appears to be an exquisitely simple strapless one-sleeve blouse is actually a stole referencing both Indian sari and Japanese origami. Accentuating the severity elegance further is the use of mannish chalkstripes – ever a Ferré favorite – to create a singular graphic effect essential to the designer’s rethinking of feminine chic. Another must in the Ferré fashion lexicon is the wide ribbon style belt with decorative topstitching and magnificent bow flourish.

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.remark: This design offers Gianfranco Ferré the opportunity to enjoy one of his favorite pastimes: playing an other-way-around game. Simply by switching front and back, the designer succeeds in turning a classic shirtwaist into a gorgeous ballgown. He conducts innovative and thoughtful research on clothing construction, then works his special magic by adding the two panels that give an appearance of volume to the skirt.

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.remark: The cape and evening gown are reminiscent of 1950s designs for opening nights at La Scala Opera House in Milan. The sumptuous style is there in the shapes, volumes and proportions. As evident in back, the dress is for the most part slim fit. The ballgown allure comes from the gorgeous material and, best of all, from the two rich paremontures (facings) gently embracing shoulders.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.description: Cape in ivory silk faille. 7/8 circle cut, capacious sleeves, no collar. Ivory beaver fur lining. Long dress in ivory silk faille. Slim line with two trumpet cut panels in front draping fluidly from shoulder and upper side seams. A wide belt in matching fabric holds the panels softly in place at waist yet only in front.

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.remark: This fabulous evening outfit sparks images of emphatic 1950s femininity. Complete with new and interesting tweaks, as in the boxy jacket versus the ballgown skirt.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.description: Jacket in boxy shape. Wide sleeves, no collar or closure. Ivory beaver fur lining. Shirt in ivory silk faille. Ample line narrowing gently toward bottom. Long sleeves. Collar and wide lapels held firmly in place at waist, no fastening. Skirt in black silk faille. Long and lavish with double construction: underneath, skinny skirt with three-button front fastening; on top, fully circular one totally open in front.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.materials: Silk faille (Taroni, Como; art. 1319, col. black & col. 908), ivory color beaver fur

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.remark: This design offers Gianfranco Ferré the opportunity to enjoy one of his favorite pastimes: playing an other-way-around game. Simply by switching front and back, the designer succeeds in turning a classic shirtwaist into a gorgeous ballgown. He conducts innovative and thoughtful research on clothing construction, then works his special magic by adding the two panels that give an appearance of volume to the skirt.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.description: Long dress in black silk faille with trompe-l’oeil construction, namely mimicking a slimline shirtwaist worn backwards. Circular panels starting at shoulders and passing firmly under the cinch belt create an overskirt effect open in front.

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.remark: Capturing the imagination here is the opulent tartan print. The use of this pattern on the bias for the front part of the skirt adds a strong element of graphic contrast to the whole.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.description: Cape in macro tartan print silk serge with full doubling in same fabric. A-line cut, floor length. Shawl collar, capacious sleeves. Skirt in macro tartan print silk serge. Slim line, floor length, front slit. A herringbone effect in front thanks to the two-section bias cut. Back on the grain in sync with the tartan pattern. Bustier in cotton piqué with allover topstitching for a stifffish feel. Short, hip skimming style. Sash in black silk taffeta.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.working_process: Horizontal 1 cm parallel topstitching all over bodice and sash

dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.remark: The architect of fashion’s DNA comes through both in the way he finesses the fabric to create the precise volume of the skirt and in the way he designs the stole to attain an emphatic geometric shape – which ultimately embraces the figure with elegance and grace.
dataset_datashack_2026/alta_moda_1986-87.description: Top in black velvet with allover topstitching. Sleeveless, (upper) hip skimming. Round neck in front, total rectangular low cut in back. Belt in black velvet with soft nappa leather covered buckle. Skirt in macro tartan print silk with all the impression of a two-part construction: underneath, a floor-length body-hugging skirt with deep front slit; on top, a richly and softly pleated panel cinched by the belt with openings front & back. Stole in rectangular cut, with one corner of the short end folded toward center and fixed in place to form a funnel style sleeve.
